description: Archived discoveries capture the validated learning behind Arrive's current quarter delivery priorities as the company converges multiple acquired businesses into one operating platform.

riskFramework.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Parking control tower convergence: validated discovery
items.name: Parking control tower convergence: validated discovery
items.summary: Discovery confirmed that parking-operator pain is less about missing features than about fragmented policy, access, and enforcement behaviors across channels and legacy estates.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether a more explicit parking control tower would improve yield, compliance, and operator trust across existing parking accounts.

linkedInitiatives.description: Parking control tower convergence initiative focused on improving parking configuration & policy management, payment channel orchestration, access, permit & LPR control, enforcement & compliance workflows, and operator analytics & control tower across Operator Control Console, Parking KPI Cockpit, and Parking Operations API.

assignments.how: Led the operator interviews and workflow synthesis that shaped the parking control tower scope.
Validated the biggest operator pain around permits, LPR, and compliance resolution.
Validated the financial and reconciliation consequences of channel fragmentation.
Helped define reusable exception and compliance workflow patterns.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Operators will expand usage if policy, payment, access, and enforcement behaviors become more coherent across their estate.
evidenceToCollect: Whether operators describe fragmentation as a top blocker to expansion.
Which parking workflows most visibly affect operator trust.

plannedActivities: Interview municipal parking operators in core markets.
Map payment, permit, access, and enforcement inconsistencies.
Define the smallest useful control-tower slice.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to prioritize convergence inside existing parking accounts before broader new-module expansion.

outcome.summary: Discovery showed strong operator pain and enough technical leverage to proceed with a parking control tower initiative.

items.title: Transit open-payment reliability: validated discovery
items.name: Transit open-payment reliability: validated discovery
items.summary: Discovery confirmed that transit buyers value open payment, but only when device-state visibility, exception handling, and revenue reconciliation are operationally trustworthy.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether a targeted reliability investment would make open-payment rollouts safer and easier to scale.

linkedInitiatives.description: Transit open-payment reliability initiative focused on improving transit fare & open payment engine, device & validator management, transit operations telemetry, and settlement, ledger & revenue reconciliation across Transit Operations Console, Validator and Driver Device Estate, and Transit Revenue Board.

assignments.how: Led operator interviews and failure-mode analysis for open-payment rollout reliability.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Transit operators will expand open-payment rollouts only if reliability concerns are addressed before network-wide deployment.
evidenceToCollect: Which reliability issues most delay rollout commitments.
What service-level confidence threshold transport executives require.

plannedActivities: Review validator and tap failure incidents in recent launches.
Map exception flows from tap to reconciliation.
Define the smallest reliability slice with visible operational benefit.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to fund reliability-first work ahead of new transport features.

outcome.summary: Discovery showed reliability risk was the main gating factor for broader open-payment expansion.

items.title: Partner launch model: validated discovery
items.name: Partner launch model: validated discovery
items.summary: Discovery confirmed that ecosystem growth is constrained more by unclear market rules, certification burden, and weak partner reporting than by raw API surface area.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether a reusable launch model would materially speed partner activation and improve ecosystem economics.

linkedInitiatives.description: Partner expansion foundation initiative focused on improving partner API, sandbox & certification, market catalog & operator rules, partner analytics & revenue reporting, automotive & in-car data services, and API gateway & developer experience across Partner APIs and SDKs, Partner Workspace, and Partner Performance Board.

assignments.how: Led partner interviews and onboarding-flow analysis across ecosystem integrations.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Partners will expand faster if they can see market rules, certify once, and trust post-launch economics.
evidenceToCollect: Where partners lose time in current market onboarding.
Which reporting gaps most reduce partner confidence after launch.

plannedActivities: Replay onboarding timelines for recently launched partners.
Map operator-rule ambiguity and certification handoffs.
Test a simplified partner launch workspace concept.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to standardize partner launch as a product workflow rather than a service-led process.

outcome.summary: Discovery showed enough onboarding friction and network upside to proceed with a reusable partner launch model.
